title: PHOTOCHEMICAL OXIDATION OF MANDELIC ACID BY BROMINE  WITH TUNGSTIC ACID SOL AS SENSITISER IN ULTRAVIOLET LIGHT OF 254 μμ.
author: J. C. GHOSH
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: A detailed investigation has been made on the oxidation of mandelic add by bromine with tongstic acid sol  as  the sensitiser in ultraviolet light of 254 μμ. The reaction is unimolecular with respect to bromine and also with respect to mandelic acid. The velocity constant passes through a maximum at pH 4.05 and it is indepen­dent of the concentration of sodium tungstate so long as the pH   is   constant. The velocity constant is proportional to the intensity of radiation absorbed by tungstic add sol. Temperature coefficient is small and the quantum efficiency is more than unity. A mechanism has been suggested.
